test_name: App route contract keeps documented SPEC route /dashboard addressable without fallback redirect
expected: Documented frontend route /dashboard remains addressable for dashboard home.
actual: Route /dashboard redirects to / via the wildcard Navigate fallback.
hypothesis: App.tsx registers legacy/current routes such as /, /charts, and /portfolios, but SPEC.md/docs/FRONTEND_API.md require /dashboard, /workspace, /portfolio, /transactions, and symbol-scoped child routes.
verification: npm run lint; npm run build; npm run test:coverage; npm run e2e; npm run e2e:integrated

test_name: Playwright smoke enforces frontend backend API boundary
expected: Browser smoke should make no requests outside frontend localhost and Spring backend /api/v1 boundary.
actual: Page load requests Google Fonts URLs from index.html and src/index.css.
hypothesis: Frontend imports remote fonts from fonts.googleapis.com in both index.html and index.css, so offline/guarded E2E runs leave the backend API boundary before user interaction.
verification: npm run lint; npm run build; npm run test:coverage; npm run e2e; npm run e2e:integrated

test_name: Playwright axe smoke has no critical accessibility violations on the mocked dashboard
expected: All interactive buttons have discernible accessible names at every configured viewport.
actual: Axe reports critical button-name violations for icon-only LeftNav action buttons in the 375px mobile viewport.
hypothesis: LeftNav hides Manage Assets and System Settings text with responsive CSS while the buttons do not provide aria-label/title fallback names.
verification: npm run lint; npm run build; npm run test:coverage; npm run e2e; npm run e2e:integrated

test_name: Vitest coverage gate with App.routes.test.tsx excluded
expected: Global branch coverage meets the configured 20% threshold while API client and utils keep stricter branch gates.
actual: Current green unit/component/API/hook subset reaches 18.79% branch coverage, below the 20% global gate. Statements 33.68%, functions 35.01%, and lines 34.08% pass their 25% thresholds.
hypothesis: Large routed views and several hooks/components remain untested or only shallowly tested, so branch-heavy UI and error paths keep global branch coverage below the initial gate.
verification: npm run lint; npm run build; npm run test:coverage; npm run e2e; npm run e2e:integrated
